class.name: GLTFFileReference
class.brief_description: Base class for referencing separate files from glTF files.
class.description: GLTFFileReference represents a reference to a separate file from the main glTF file. These could be images, binary blob buffers, audio files, other glTF files, or any other type of file. File references may point to external files, embedded files in buffer views, or embedded files in data URIs.

method.name: create_missing_directories_if_needed
method.description: Creates any missing directories in the filesystem needed for writing to the external file URI specified by this file reference's [member file_uri]. If [member file_uri] is an empty string, this does nothing and returns [constant @GlobalScope.OK]. If the directories already exist, this does nothing and returns [constant @GlobalScope.OK]. If the directories do not exist, it attempts to create them and returns [constant @GlobalScope.OK] on success, or an error code on failure.

method.name: read_file_data
method.description: Loads the file data referenced by this file reference, either from the buffer view or the external URI, and returns it as a [PackedByteArray]. If both a buffer view and an external URI are specified, the external URI takes precedence if the file exists. If neither is specified, or if loading fails, an error is printed and an empty [PackedByteArray] is returned.

method.name: read_file_reference_entries_from_dictionary
method.description: Reads general glTF file reference properties from the given [Dictionary] and apply it to this file reference object.

method.name: write_file_data
method.description: Writes the given [param data] into either the external file URI, if [member file_uri] is set, or into a new buffer view in the given [GLTFState] if [member file_uri] is an empty string. The [param alignment], [param deduplicate], and [param buffer_index] parameters are only used when writing to a new buffer view, and are directly passed to the [method GLTFBufferView.write_new_buffer_view_into_state] function. They do nothing when writing to an external file URI.

method.name: write_file_reference_entries_to_dictionary
method.description: Base function for converting file references to a [Dictionary]. If implementing a custom glTF file reference in script, this can be used as the start of a [code]to_dictionary[/code] function.

members.member: The index of the [GLTFBufferView] that contains the data for this file reference. If this is set to [code]-1[/code], then the file reference does not refer to a buffer view. This property is mutually exclusive with [member file_uri], so if this is set to a value other than [code]-1[/code], then [member file_uri] SHOULD be an empty string. If both are set, [member file_uri] takes precedence when the file exists.

The URI of the file relative to the glTF file's location. If this is an empty string, then the file reference does not refer to an external file. This property is mutually exclusive with [member buffer_view_index], so if this is set to a non-empty string, then [member buffer_view_index] SHOULD be [code]-1[/code]. If both are set, [member file_uri] takes precedence when the file exists. File names SHOULD use snake case and all lowercase letters to avoid case sensitivity issues across platforms.

The MIME type of the file being referenced. This is a required property, and must be a valid MIME type string, such as [code]"image/png"[/code] for PNG images. When a file format's media type is registered with IANA, this MUST match the media type string as registered with IANA. When a file format's media type is not registered with IANA, any placeholder name may be used pending registration.
